title: calceolaria palustris subsp. micrantha
intro: Calceolaria palustris subsp. micrantha, also known as the marsh slipper or small-flowered calceolaria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Calceolaria palustris subsp. micrantha include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
